commit.message:
Multi-interpolator pipeline + homotopy complex-projection fix

Multi-interpolator refactor:
- Add interpolators field to EstimationOptions for running multiple
  interpolation methods in a single call (shared SI template/SIAN
  analysis, per-interpolator HC solve)
- Add InterpolatorAGPRobustRQ, InterpolatorAGPRobustSEpRQ,
  InterpolatorAGPRobustSExRQ kernel variants
- Add interpolator_source field to ParameterEstimationResult to tag
  which interpolator produced each result
- Extract setup_identifiability() for shared identifiability-only setup
- Add resolve_interpolator_list(), interpolator_method_to_symbol() helpers
- Default try_more_methods to false (ignored when interpolators list is set)
- Backward-compatible: empty interpolators falls back to single interpolator field

Homotopy fix:
- Fix solve_with_hc_parameterized dropping all solutions at shooting
  points where all roots are complex. Add fallback that projects complex
  solutions to real parts (matching existing solve_with_hc behavior).
  Verified: Solo Hom 16→32, Multi Hom 79→147, matching NoHom exactly.

Kernel improvements in derivatives.jl:
- Add kernel_type parameter to agp_gpr_robust for RQ, SE+RQ, SE*RQ kernels
- Improve GP hyperparameter optimization robustness

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: handle Raku sprintf -0 flag combo for float specifiers (#815)

In Raku, when both '-' and '0' flags are present in float format
specifiers (%f/%F/%e/%E), zero-padding takes priority over left-align
(unlike C where '-' overrides '0'). Additionally, when the number has
no sign prefix, one leading zero is shifted from the integer part to
the fractional part. This matches Rakudo's behavior and passes all
2282 tests in roast/6.d/S32-str/sprintf-f.t.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Bring all notebook sections to constraint_sensitivity standard

Each section now has:
- "The mystery" opening: states what the SM cannot explain and why it matters
- Named derivation section: numbered steps with geometric sources
- "Reading the plots" closing paragraph: explains each plot's content and
  how the two plots in the pair relate to each other
- Inline code comments on every formula explaining its physics

Section-specific improvements:
- §2 PMNS: explains maximal mixing mystery, Z₂ eigenvector derivation,
  heatmap annotated with sin²θ₂₃ box and "identical rows" note,
  bar chart annotated with exact fractions
- §3 H₀: full Hubble tension context (5σ, both methods, no consensus
  fix), formula motivation, right plot shows CMB inconsistency via
  double-headed arrow marking the ΛCDM bias gap
- §4 α_w: all three SM gauge couplings stated as free parameters,
  SU(2)/RP³ identification explained step by step, left diagram uses
  color-coded boxes per step, right bar chart adds PPM vs observed
  bracket with percent-error label

feat(gateway): flag-until-fixed — re-propose manual_required issues each scan

ExecutionGateway.get_unresolved_proposals(): returns (ProposedAction, record)
pairs for all manual_required records — APPROVED verdicts with no IaC path
that still need a human to fix the Azure resource manually.

_run_agent_scan(): after agent.scan() returns, merges unresolved proposals for
resources not already in the current scan. Deduplicates by (resource_id,
action_type). Each re-flagged action's reason is prefixed with
"[Unresolved since DD Mon]" so the live log is explicit about why it appeared.

Re-flagging stops when:
  - Human clicks Dismiss in dashboard (dismissed status excluded)
  - The underlying Azure config changes (agent naturally stops proposing)
  - A PR is open (pr_created excluded — don't create duplicates)
  - Issue is escalated (awaiting_review excluded — HITL panel covers it)

tests/test_execution_gateway.py: TestUnresolvedProposals (7 tests).
Tests: 551 passed, 0 failed.

feat: cross-room CO promotion via supervisor lifecycle messages

When the supervisor dismisses/joins a bot, its reply message
(e.g. "HERACLES: Cid stopped") is parsed by all bots in the room
to update the CO roster in real-time. No separate signal mechanism
needed — the supervisor already sends these messages via intercom.

- main.ts: handleLifecycleMessage() parses supervisor messages,
  updates roomRoster, calls rerankCO for promotion/demotion
- supervisor.ts: includes rank in "started" messages, updates
  local presence file after lifecycle actions
- service.ts: exports updatePresence() for supervisor use

Add database export functionality (#95)

* Add "Export database" to File menu with database.export usecase

Adds a full-stack export database feature:
- Backend interface: new `database.export(path)` method
- DataRepositories: new `export(path)` method
- DatabaseExport usecase in executing-backend
- SQLite implementation: opens a separate read-only connection and uses
  VACUUM INTO to create a backup at the given path
- Demo implementation: serializes data to JSON, triggers browser download
  or writes to file in Node.js context
- Electron File menu: "Export database" item with native save dialog
- IPC proxy wiring for both server and client

https://claude.ai/code/session_01PPv59bnas1XYiiHWChMeNj

* Use path's last segment as download filename in demo export

https://claude.ai/code/session_01PPv59bnas1XYiiHWChMeNj

* Remove Node.js fs fallback from demo export — always in browser

https://claude.ai/code/session_01PPv59bnas1XYiiHWChMeNj

* Use node:sqlite backup API instead of VACUUM INTO

https://claude.ai/code/session_01PPv59bnas1XYiiHWChMeNj

* Extract exportDatabase to own file, remove from devenv menu

Make onExportDatabase optional in ActionHandlers and filter it out of
the File menu when not provided. Devenv mode no longer shows the item.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01PPv59bnas1XYiiHWChMeNj

* fix: fixes to vibed code

* chore: update es-toolkit

commit.message:
Polish puzzle rendering, interaction, and image pipeline

- Two-pass quantization: de-prioritize small regions in palette selection
- Mode filter replaces blur for sharper color boundaries
- Compact palette: renumber to remove gaps after region merging
- Puzzle canvas at 1024px resolution (matches DALL-E output)
- Mouse drag to pan; two-finger touch for pan+zoom; MIN_SCALE=1 (no zoom-out past 100%)
- Auto-reset transform when scale returns to 1
- Canvas always fills wrap width (no side bars)
- Fullscreen toggle button on all screens
- Store indexMap in IDB for exact session restore
- Store/restore preview blob across refreshes
- Style prompt: painterly, realistic, avoid cartoon shading and fine linework
- Portrait/landscape image sizing based on viewport aspect ratio
- API key hidden behind toggle button with checkmark indicator
- Palette renumbering, phantom checkmark fix, region merge improvements

commit.message:
fix: instant logout — clear user before signOut network call

Previously logout() set isLoading=true before the signOut() request,
showing the 'Connecting...' spinner for the full round-trip duration.
User state is now cleared immediately so the redirect to /login is
instant. The signOut() call still runs in the background to invalidate
the server session.

commit.message:
Fix tool switching — decouple selectbox from session state key

Streamlit's widget-bound keys can't be set programmatically and
persist in the internal widget registry even after deletion.
Fix: track current_tool as a plain session state variable (no key=
on selectbox), read it as the default index. Now Generate button
in Matrise reliably switches to AEO Agent.

fix: convert streamed jobs to interactive card on backend timeout

When /api/chat/send returns 502 (Railway timeout) but the WebSocket
already streamed job results, the LiveJobStreamPanel was stuck showing
a non-interactive list. Now on error, if stream data exists, converts
the unique jobs into a fallback JobResultsCard with full expand/collapse
and action buttons so users can still browse and apply to jobs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

🐛 Fix /demo proxy: strip hop-by-hop headers, fix port kill bug

- dev.rs: strip hop-by-hop headers (transfer-encoding, connection,
  keep-alive, etc.) when proxying to/from Next.js; forwarding
  transfer-encoding: chunked caused axum to send an invalid response
  and drop the connection (empty reply to client)
- tmuxy-demo/package.json: hardcode port 9002 in predev kill-port
  script; using ${PORT:-9002} was resolving to PORT=9000 (the main
  server port), killing the Rust server on every dev restart
